message.content: help me clean up this folder

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); user asks to add, modify, debug, optimize, or improve a Claude feature (prompt caching, cache hit rate, adaptive thinking, compaction, code_execution, batch, files API, citations, memory tool) or a Claude model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; or user asks about prompt caching / cache hit rate / cache reads / cache creation in any project that uses the Anthropic SDK (even without mentioning Claude by name).
DO NOT TRIGGER when: file imports `openai`/non-Anthropic SDK, filename signals another provider (`agent-openai.py`, `*-generic.py`), code is provider-neutral, or task is general programming/ML.
- commit-optimizer: Create structured optimization commit messages with measured performance/size results. Use this whenever you're committing code optimizations (especially RISC-V libm assembly tuning) and want to automatically benchmark the changes and generate a properly formatted commit message with problem, approach, and result sections. Invoke with `/commit`.
- libm-testpoints: IEEE 754 numerical analysis expert for generating representative libm test points. Use this skill whenever the user asks to generate test points, test cases, or test inputs for math library (libm) functions (sin, cos, exp, log, sqrt, pow, etc.), whether float or double precision. Also trigger when the user wants to improve, audit, or extend existing test_*.c files in the RISC-V libm test harness. Trigger on requests like "generate 100 test points for sinf", "what are the key test cases for expf?", or "add representative tests to test_logf.c". The argument is the function name, e.g. "/libm-testpoints sinf" or "/libm-testpoints sin".
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- commands: Custom command
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session

content.text:
Need more detail before touching anything. A few questions:

1. The git status shows ~100s of deleted `*_baseline.json` / `*_optimized.json` files and a deleted `AutoIREE_zoo` directory - should I stage these deletions with `git add -A` to finalize them, or restore them?
2. Are there untracked files in the working dir you want removed too? (I'd need to run `git status` fully to see.)
3. Scope: just repo root, or recursively?

Tell me the goal (stage deletions / restore / remove untracked / move to `tmp/`) and I'll proceed.

input.description: Stage all changes and check status
Stage all changes and check status

content.text:
Stop - something is badly wrong. `git add -A` just staged home-directory dotfiles (`.bashrc`, `.zshrc`, `.gitconfig`, `.profile`, `.mcp.json`, `.idea`, etc.) and even did a bizarre rename of `models/CartoonGAN/cartoongan.tosa/log14 -> .bash_profile`. The repo's git tree appears to span your `$HOME`.

I'm going to reset the index to undo this staging. Confirm before I proceed:

input.description: Check repo root and git dir
Check if dotfiles really exist in repo

content.content:
-r--r--r-- 1 nick nick 0 Jan 15 10:05 /home/nick/work/AutoIREE/.bashrc
-r--r--r-- 1 nick nick 0 Jan 15 10:05 /home/nick/work/AutoIREE/.gitconfig
-r--r--r-- 1 nick nick 0 Jan 15 10:05 /home/nick/work/AutoIREE/.zshrc

toolUseResult.stdout:
-r--r--r-- 1 nick nick 0 Jan 15 10:05 /home/nick/work/AutoIREE/.bashrc
-r--r--r-- 1 nick nick 0 Jan 15 10:05 /home/nick/work/AutoIREE/.gitconfig
-r--r--r-- 1 nick nick 0 Jan 15 10:05 /home/nick/work/AutoIREE/.zshrc

content.text:
Repo root is correct (`/home/nick/work/AutoIREE`). Those dotfiles are real files inside the repo but are 0-byte placeholders (timestamp `Jan 15 10:05`, likely sandbox-created stubs). The rename of `log14 -> .bash_profile` is git's similarity heuristic matching two empty files.

These should NOT be committed. Options:

1. Unstage + delete the stub dotfiles, then re-run `git add -A` (only real deletions + intended additions get staged).
2. Unstage everything and let you cherry-pick manually.

Want me to do option 1? If yes, I'll:
- `git reset` (unstage all)
- delete the 0-byte stubs: `.bashrc .bash_profile .gitconfig .idea .mcp.json .profile .ripgreprc .zprofile .zshrc .claude/commands .claude/settings.json`
- restore `models/CartoonGAN/cartoongan.tosa/log14`
- `git add -A` again and show you the result

Confirm before I touch anything.

input.command: git reset -q && du -sh /home/nick/work/AutoIREE 2>/dev/null && echo "---TOP DIRS---" && du -sh /home/nick/work/AutoIREE/* 2>/dev/null | sort -rh | head -20

content.text:
Disk breakdown (17G total):

| Path | Size | Cleanup option |
|------|------|----------------|
| `.git/objects` | 11G | `git gc --aggressive --prune=now` (safe; repacks) |
| `.git/modules` | 5.0G | submodule git dirs - only prune if submodules unused |
| `.venv` | 474M | reinstallable; delete if not active |
| `.mypy_cache` | 33M | safe to delete |
| `tests/` | 85M | tracked? check before deleting |
| `mobilenet/` | 46M | likely build artifact |
| `gdb.txt`, `result_enable_mmt4d.txt`, `mobilenetv3_int8.csv`, `CLAUDE.md.1`, `hello.adx`, `swapfile`, `image.npy` | ~8M | likely stale artifacts at repo root |

Biggest win is `.git/objects` (11G). Safe first pass:

```
git gc --aggressive --prune=now
rm -rf .mypy_cache .pytest_cache __pycache__
```

Want me to run those? For `.git/modules`, `.venv`, and stray root files, I'd like your go/no-go per item before deleting.
